heading: Employment and benefits protection
label: 2614 Employment and benefits protection
notes: Amendments2008—Subsec. (c)(2)(B)(i). , inserted “or under ” before semicolon.Pub. L. 110–181, § 585(a)(3)(F)(i)section 2612(a)(3) of this titleSubsec. (c)(3)(A)(iii). , added cl. (iii).Pub. L. 110–181, § 585(a)(3)(F)(ii)

L. 103–3section 2601 of this title
amendments: Amendments2008—Subsec. (c)(2)(B)(i). , inserted “or under ” before semicolon.Pub. L. 110–181, § 585(a)(3)(F)(i)section 2612(a)(3) of this titleSubsec. (c)(3)(A)(iii). , added cl. (iii).Pub. L. 110–181, § 585(a)(3)(F)(ii)

children.label: A
children.text: to be restored by the employer to the position of employment held by the employee when the leave commenced; or

children.label: B
children.text: to be restored to an equivalent position with equivalent employment benefits, pay, and other terms and conditions of employment.

children.heading: Loss of benefits
children.label: 2 Loss of benefits
children.text: The taking of leave under shall not result in the loss of any employment benefit accrued prior to the date on which the leave commenced.section 2612 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the accrual of any seniority or employment benefits during any period of leave; or

children.label: B
children.text: any right, benefit, or position of employment other than any right, benefit, or position to which the employee would have been entitled had the employee not taken the leave.

children.heading: Certification
children.label: 4 Certification
children.text: As a condition of restoration under paragraph (1) for an employee who has taken leave under , the employer may have a uniformly applied practice or policy that requires each such employee to receive certification from the health care provider of the employee that the employee is able to resume work, except that nothing in this paragraph shall supersede a valid State or local law or a collective bargaining agreement that governs the return to work of such employees.section 2612(a)(1)(D) of this title

children.heading: Construction
children.label: 5 Construction
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to prohibit an employer from requiring an employee on leave under to report periodically to the employer on the status and intention of the employee to return to work.section 2612 of this title

children.heading: Exemption concerning certain highly compensated employees
children.label: b Exemption concerning certain highly compensated employees
A
children.text: such denial is necessary to prevent substantial and grievous economic injury to the operations of the employer;

children.label: B
children.text: the employer notifies the employee of the intent of the employer to deny restoration on such basis at the time the employer determines that such injury would occur; and

children.label: C
children.text: in any case in which the leave has commenced, the employee elects not to return to employment after receiving such notice.

children.heading: Affected employees
children.label: 2 Affected employees
children.text: An eligible employee described in paragraph (1) is a salaried eligible employee who is among the highest paid 10 percent of the employees employed by the employer within 75 miles of the facility at which the employee is employed.

children.heading: Coverage
children.label: 1 Coverage
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), during any period that an eligible employee takes leave under , the employer shall maintain coverage under any “group health plan” (as defined in ) for the duration of such leave at the level and under the conditions coverage would have been provided if the employee had continued in employment continuously for the duration of such leave.section 2612 of this titlesection 5000(b)(1) of title 26

children.label: A
children.text: the employee fails to return from leave under after the period of leave to which the employee is entitled has expired; andsection 2612 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the continuation, recurrence, or onset of a serious health condition that entitles the employee to leave under subparagraph (C) or (D) of or under ; orsection 2612(a)(1) of this titlesection 2612(a)(3) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: other circumstances beyond the control of the employee.

children.label: i
children.text: a certification issued by the health care provider of the son, daughter, spouse, or parent of the employee, as appropriate, in the case of an employee unable to return to work because of a condition specified in ;section 2612(a)(1)(C) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: a certification issued by the health care provider of the eligible employee, in the case of an employee unable to return to work because of a condition specified in ; orsection 2612(a)(1)(D) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: a certification issued by the health care provider of the servicemember being cared for by the employee, in the case of an employee unable to return to work because of a condition specified in .section 2612(a)(3) of this title

children.heading: Copy
children.label: B Copy
children.text: The employee shall provide, in a timely manner, a copy of such certification to the employer.

children.heading: Leave due to serious health condition of employee
children.label: i Leave due to serious health condition of employee
children.text: The certification described in subparagraph (A)(ii) shall be sufficient if the certification states that a serious health condition prevented the employee from being able to perform the functions of the position of the employee on the date that the leave of the employee expired.

children.heading: Leave due to serious health condition of family member
children.label: ii Leave due to serious health condition of family member
children.text: The certification described in subparagraph (A)(i) shall be sufficient if the certification states that the employee is needed to care for the son, daughter, spouse, or parent who has a serious health condition on the date that the leave of the employee expired.
